success_surface: Complex column splitting with irregular delimiters and style preservation across variable-length data rows
effective_behaviors: Performed multi-modal inspection using both pandas for quick overviews and openpyxl for precise cell-level structure understanding
Identified exact target row/column ranges from task metadata before implementing solution logic
Applied delimiter-based splitting while preserving all segment content including special characters like standalone dashes
Correctly handled openpyxl style copying by extracting individual style components rather than assigning StyleProxy objects directly
Implemented boundary checking to prevent writes beyond target column limits
Filled unused target cells within range with required formatting even when data has fewer segments than destination columns
Applied consistent styling (fill color, font, alignment) across all processed cells including headers
Output verification logs showing source values and split results to confirm correctness
evidence: Initial exploration used both pandas.read_excel() and load_workbook() to understand sheet structure
Cell-by-cell inspection loop printed coordinates and values before implementation
Error handling: First attempt assigned prev_cell.alignment causing TypeError; corrected to use copy() on individual components
Range limit check: 'if target_col <= 13' prevented out-of-bounds writes
Remaining column loop filled unpopulated cells with light green shading through range(len(segments) + 3, 14)
Header row (row 2) was also formatted with same shading and alignment
Final print statements showed source column A values alongside split C-M values for each processed row

memory_items.title: Dual-Tool Spreadsheet Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Use both high-level (pandas) and low-level (openpyxl) tools to fully understand spreadsheet structure before manipulating
memory_items.generalization: When dealing with complex layouts or mixed data/types, combine DataFrame summaries with direct cell coordinate inspection to capture all structural details.

memory_items.title: Safe Style Component Copying in openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: Do not directly assign StyleProxy objects between cells; extract and copy individual style components instead
memory_items.generalization: When inheriting formatting in openpyxl, use copy() on specific properties (font, border, alignment, fill, number_format) rather than assigning entire style objects to avoid TypeErrors.

memory_items.title: Delimiter Splitting with Boundary Protection
memory_items.lesson: Split strings by specified delimiter and distribute across target cells while checking column bounds on each write operation
memory_items.generalization: For column-splitting operations, always validate target column indices against maximum allowed before assignment, ensuring no overflow beyond intended range.

memory_items.title: Complete Range Coverage Even with Partial Data
memory_items.lesson: Apply required formatting to all cells in the target range, including those without corresponding data segments
memory_items.generalization: When distributing split data across a fixed column range, fill any remaining cells within the range with appropriate formatting (e.g., background colors) even if they contain no data.

memory_items.title: Verification Logging for Data Manipulation Tasks
memory_items.lesson: Print verification output after manipulation showing source values alongside transformed results
memory_items.generalization: After executing spreadsheet transformations, log both input and output states to verify correctness and aid debugging if issues arise.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons

### Complex Column Operations
- **Dual-Tool Inspection**: Use both pandas and openpyxl together—pandas for quick overviews of data content, openpyxl for precise cell coordinate structure and formatting details.
- **Safe Style Inheritance**: Never assign `cell.style` or style components directly from another cell's StyleProxy object. Always use `copy()` on individual components (`font`, `border`, `alignment`, `fill`, `number_format`) before assignment.
- **Split-and-Fill Pattern**: When splitting one column into multiple target columns:
  1. Split the source string by the specified delimiter
  2. Write each segment to consecutive columns starting from the target start
  3. Check boundary conditions before each write (`target_col <= max_column`)
  4. Apply required formatting to ALL cells in the target range, including those with no data
- **Boundary-Aware Distribution**: Always validate that calculated target column indices don't exceed the worksheet's or task-specified maximum column before attempting to write.
- **Verification Protocol**: After completing data transformation, print verification output showing source values alongside their distributed results for manual confirmation.
